summary: Iteration 3 testing complete. Backend: 19/19 pytest pass (PUT /groups, /groups/me objetivo_mensual, POST /products categoria + default 'Otros', PUT /products perms, DELETE /products 403 for admin_menor/member, /sales/export.csv headers+columns, /dashboard new fields + vendedor_id filter). Frontend: Onboarding/Inventory/Members all working — objetivo save, category select on add, search input, cat-* chips, edit modal, delete via menu, member role-gating (no menu-* for members, no input-objetivo for members), vendor-* chips after a second member joins. CRITICAL: Sales page crashes entirely due to missing imports — export-csv, input-search-sales unreachable.

ui_bugs.issue: Page crashes with React ErrorBoundary: missing imports — `useMemo` not imported from React (line 29), `API` constant not imported from ../lib/api (line 40), `Search` icon (line 96) and `Download` icon (line 79) not imported from lucide-react. Result: entire Sales page renders blank/black; export-csv button and input-search-sales never mount.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot be exercised because Sales component throws on render. Backend /api/sales/export.csv works (verified by pytest).

issues: Recharts width(-1)/height(-1) warning still emitted briefly on first mount (non-blocking, carried over from iter 1-2).

action_items: Fix /app/frontend/src/pages/Sales.js imports: add `useMemo` to React import; add `import { API } from '../lib/api';` (or destructure from default import); add `Search` and `Download` to lucide-react imports.
Re-test Sales page after fix: confirm sales-list renders, export-csv downloads ventas_YYYY-MM-DD.csv blob, input-search-sales filters by product name and seller name.
critical_code_review_comments: Sales.js uses `useMemo`, `API`, `Search`, `Download` without importing them — this is a pre-merge oversight. A simple ESLint/no-undef pass would have caught this. Recommend adding lint to the CI / pre-commit.

Sales.js exportCsv hits `${API}/sales/export.csv` with manual fetch + Authorization header; the rest of the app uses the `api` axios client. For consistency and to share interceptors, prefer api.get with responseType: 'blob'.
server.py is now ~870 lines; growing past the 700-line guideline. Split into modules (auth, groups, members, products, sales, incidents, files, dashboard) — flagged in iter 2 still pending.
/api/dashboard re-fetches and iterates all_products multiple times in Python for every bucket; could be a single Mongo $facet aggregation as the dataset grows.

CSV export builds rows with simple .replace('"',"'") quoting — does not escape embedded newlines or commas in unquoted columns (Fecha/Categoría/numeric cols are safe; producto/vendedor are quoted). Consider Python's csv module for safety.
POST /products still loops insert_one N times when cantidad>1 — recommend insert_many (flagged in iter 2).

success_rate.frontend: ~85% — all flows OK except Sales page entirely broken (export-csv + input-search-sales blocked)

seed_data_creation: Per-run users + group TEST_IT3_Group; group is cascade-deleted at end of test_iteration3.py (test_cleanup_delete_group). Frontend playwright test creates TEST_IT3_FE group + two users (it3_a_*, it3_b_*) — left in DB (no cleanup endpoint exercised in UI flow).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter3 backend tests: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py — single-file pytest module, scenario fixture (creator A + member B + admin_menor C + admin_total D). Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py -v. The Sales.js missing-imports bug is the only blocker — once fixed, retest export-csv (Blob download) and input-search-sales filter. Dashboard vendor-* chips correctly only appear when members.length>1 (verified by registering UserB and joining same group). Members.input-objetivo correctly hidden for non-admin.
rca of the issue: Sales.js missing imports — the file uses `useMemo`, `API`, `Search`, `Download` identifiers that are never imported. React's error boundary catches the ReferenceError at render time and unmounts the component, leaving the page blank with a console warning 'An error occurred in the <Sales> component'. Reproduction: log in, register a sale, navigate to /app/ventas. Mitigation: add the missing imports in /app/frontend/src/pages/Sales.js lines 1-3 — `import React, { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from 'react';` `import { Receipt, AlertCircle, Loader2, Trophy, BarChart3, Search, Download } from 'lucide-react';` and `import api, { API, buildFileUrl } from '../lib/api';`.
